note: Mississippi has NOT expanded Medicaid. Working-age adults without dependent children generally do not qualify for Medicaid unless they have a disability.

programs.name: SNAP (Mississippi)
programs.description: Mississippi administers federal SNAP with standard federal eligibility rules.

monthly_benefit_range.note: Standard federal benefit amounts (FY2025 maximum allotments)

income.note: Federal gross income limit; net income must be at or below 100% FPL

programs.name: Mississippi Medicaid
programs.description: Mississippi has NOT expanded Medicaid. Coverage for non-disabled adults without children is very limited. Children, pregnant women, the elderly, and people with disabilities may qualify (CHIP up to 209% FPL).

monthly_benefit_range.note: No premium for most enrollees. Covers doctor visits, hospital, prescriptions, and more.

income.adults_note: No Medicaid expansion; eligibility for adults is very narrow

required_documents: Social Security number or immigration documents

programs.name: TANF (Mississippi)
programs.full_name: TANF (Mississippi) — Temporary Assistance for Needy Families
programs.description: Mississippi's TANF program provides time-limited cash assistance to low-income families with children. Maximum monthly benefit for a family of 3 is approximately $170.

eligibility_rules.who: Families with dependent children under 18 (or 19 if still in high school)

income.note: Very low income limits; varies by family size. Generally well below 100% FPL.

eligibility_rules.time_limit: 60-month lifetime limit (federal); some states have shorter limits
